concept: Peaceful / At Peace / Free from Conflict
description: Free from disturbance, conflict, or agitation; characterized by peace. 'Phaelu' names the quality Phi seeks to embody—the state of being where harmony prevails, where violence has no purchase, where presence is undisturbed.
sound_symbolism: The word breathes peace itself. 'Pha' /ɸa/ begins with soft breath, non-violent presence. The vowel hiatus 'a.e' opens spaciousness, room for all. Then 'lu' settles into liquid rest, peace embodied.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'phaelu miona' (peaceful person). Core vocabulary for Phi's peace-centered philosophy. Can modify any noun to indicate its peaceful quality or state. The noun is 'shea' (peace); 'shena' (calm) names stillness of the moment and 'noalu' (tranquil) the deeper settled form — 'phaelu' is the abiding disposition toward peace.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Inner peace (shanti) is both path and destination—the peaceful mind sees clearly and acts wisely.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Peacefulness is Phi's core quality—the language exists to cultivate and express this state of being.
